sources.notes: Compute the expected square of the number of palindromic subsegments for random colors; easy version has n <= 2000.
Official tutorial is sparse on the page, but the intended tags and statement point to pairwise expectation over palindromic-subsegment indicators.

annotation.summary: For a random length-n string over m colors, compute the expected square of the number of palindromic substrings.
annotation.constraints: In the easy version, total n is at most 2000, allowing direct or near-direct enumeration of pairs of substrings with modular probabilities.
annotation.core_idea: Let correctness be the sum of indicator variables over all substrings being palindromes. Its square expands into a sum over ordered pairs of substrings; for each pair, build the equality constraints induced by both palindrome conditions and add m^{-rank} modulo p. The easy bound permits enumerating the relevant pairs directly.
annotation.complexity: Polynomial in n for the easy version, using pair enumeration and constraint counting.
tricks: expand square of a count into pairs of indicators
count equality components induced by palindrome constraints

solution_variants.name: pairwise-indicator-expectation
solution_variants.summary: Use linearity of expectation on all ordered pairs of palindromic-substring indicators.

tags.evidence: The square of correctness is evaluated by summing probabilities over pairs of indicator variables.
tags.description: Use linearity of expectation to sum contributions of indicator variables or pairs of indicators.
tags.created_reason: Expectation problems are common enough to distinguish this from the broad probability tag.

tags.evidence: Each term counts color assignments satisfying equality constraints.
A direct implementation can count equality components induced by palindrome constraints with DSU.
